truth.papers.A78.title: Microstructural Modulation through Process Control Leading to Ultrastrong Ductile Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% Low-Density Steel
truth.papers.A78.paper_scope: Investigates microstructural modulation through process control (hot rolling, cold rolling, annealing) to achieve ultrastrong ductile low-density Fe-10Al-15Mn-0.8C-5Ni steel; focuses on B2 intermetallic strengthening, fracture behavior, and comparison with TRIP steel toughness.

prediction.papers.A78.title: Microstructural Modulation through Process Control Leading to Ultrastrong Ductile Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% Low-Density Steel
prediction.papers.A78.paper_scope: Development of ultrastrong ductile low-density Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni steel through hot rolling, cold rolling, and annealing, focusing on B2 intermetallic phase control to achieve nearly 1 GPa yield strength and high toughness comparable to TRIP steel.

truth.processes.proc_as_cast.description: Vacuum arc melting and solidification, resulting in as-cast microstructure with 27% δ-ferrite.
truth.processes.proc_as_cast.processes_notes: Ingot remelted 5-6 times inside arc melter without breaking vacuum for elemental intermixing.
truth.processes.proc_HR.description: Homogenization at 1150°C for 2 h followed by hot rolling from 14 mm to 3 mm in 9 passes at 1050–900°C, air-cooled.
truth.processes.proc_HR.processes_notes: Finish rolling temperature ~900°C; air-cooled HR sheet.
truth.processes.proc_CR.description: Hot rolling (14→3 mm) and subsequent cold rolling from 3 mm to 2 mm at room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_anneal_reg.description: Conventional hot rolling (14→3 mm, 9 passes, 1050–900°C, air-cooled) and cold rolling (3→2 mm) followed by annealing at 900°C for 5–30 min, water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_anneal_reg.processes_note: Annealing time varied: 5, 10, 15, 30 min; water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_anneal_mod.description: High-speed hot rolling (10 rpm, 14→~2 mm) and cold rolling (1 mm reduction) followed by annealing at 900°C for 5–30 min, water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_anneal_mod.processes_note: Modified HR with 10 rpm vs 1 rpm in conventional; CR reduction of 1 mm; annealing times 5, 15, 30 min used for mechanical tests.

prediction.processes.proc_fe10al_cast.description: As-cast condition after vacuum arc melting and solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_fe10al_cast.processes_not: Vacuum arc melting at 10^-5 mbar backfilled with argon; ingot remelted 5–6 times without breaking vacuum. As-cast microstructure contains approximately 27% δ-ferrite.
prediction.processes.proc_fe10al_std.description: Standard processing route: homogenization at 1150 °C for 2 h, hot rolling at 1050 °C from 14 mm to 3 mm by nine passes to finish rolling temperature 900 °C with air cooling, cold rolling from 3 mm to 2 mm at room temperature, and annealing at 900 °C for 5–30 min followed by water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_fe10al_std.processes_note: Samples designated A5, A10, A15, and A30 based on annealing time. B2 stringer band volume fraction approximately 16% in annealed conditions.
prediction.processes.proc_fe10al_mod.description: Modified processing route: homogenization at 1150 °C for 2 h, high-speed hot rolling at 10 rpm reducing plate thickness from 14 mm to approximately 2 mm (slightly more) to finish rolling temperature approximately 900 °C with air cooling, cold rolling with 1 mm thickness reduction at room temperature, and annealing at 900 °C for 5–30 min followed by water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_fe10al_mod.processes_note: Higher speed HR refines B2 stringer bands, reducing their volume fraction from approximately 16% to 11%. Samples also designated A5, A15, and A30 based on annealing time.

truth.processing_steps.as_cast.processing_step: Ingot remelted 5-6 times for better elemental intermixing; differential melting points addressed; solidified as-cast retained 27% δ-ferrite.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_cast.proc: Individually melted elements at 10^-5 mbar backfilled with argon; ingot remelted 5–6 times without breaking vacuum
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_std_hr.pr: Nine passes in a 75-ton rolling mill from 14 mm to 3 mm; finish rolling temperature 900 °C; air-cooled
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_std_cr.pr: Thickness reduced from 3 mm to 2 mm
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_std_a10.p: Preceded by casting, homogenization 1150 °C 2 h, HR 1050 °C nine passes 14→3 mm to 900 °C, and CR 3→2 mm
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_std_a15.p: Preceded by casting, homogenization 1150 °C 2 h, HR 1050 °C nine passes 14→3 mm to 900 °C, and CR 3→2 mm
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_std_a30.p: Preceded by casting, homogenization 1150 °C 2 h, HR 1050 °C nine passes 14→3 mm to 900 °C, and CR 3→2 mm
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_mod_a15.p: Preceded by casting, homogenization 1150 °C 2 h, high-speed HR 10 rpm 14→≈2 mm, and CR 1 mm reduction
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe10al_mod_a30.p: Preceded by casting, homogenization 1150 °C 2 h, high-speed HR 10 rpm 14→≈2 mm, and CR 1 mm reduction

truth.structures.as_cast.overall_structure: δ-ferrite in austenite (γ) matrix; 27% δ-ferrite retained at room temperature due to incomplete peritectic reaction.
truth.structures.HR.overall_structure: Transformed from as-cast: δ-ferrite transformed into lamellar B2 stringer bands within austenite matrix; δ-ferrite fraction reduced to ~19%.
truth.structures.HR.microstructure_list[2].pre: nucleated at shear bands in γ
truth.structures.CR.overall_structure: Further reduction by cold rolling: pancaking of γ, thinning of B2 stringer bands, shape deformation of IG-B2.
truth.structures.reg_A5.overall_structure: After annealing at 900°C: austenite matrix with B2 stringer bands, IG-B2 and GB-B2 precipitates. Some γ recrystallized (RE-γ), some unrecrystallized (URE-γ).
truth.structures.reg_A5.microstructure_counts: B2 stringer bands ~16%, IG-B2 ~3.7% total (2.6% in URE-γ, 1.1% in RE-γ), GB-B2 ~16%? (not specified)
truth.structures.reg_A5.microstructure_list[4]: lamellar stringer bands, bamboo-like after recrystallization
grain boundaries in B2 stringer; EBSD shows orientation spread
needle-shaped in URE-γ (0.2 μm), spherical-type in RE-γ (0.29 μm)
coarse, irregular, nucleated along prior γ grain boundaries
at prior γ grain boundaries, decorating boundaries
truth.structures.reg_A10.overall_structure: Similar to A5 but with larger recrystallized γ grains (RE-γ) and coarser precipitates.
truth.structures.reg_A15.overall_structure: Further growth of precipitates and grain size.
truth.structures.reg_A30.overall_structure: B2 stringer bands, larger IG-B2 and GB-B2; annealing twins present; IG-B2 coarsened into needle shape through mutual coalescence.
truth.structures.reg_A30.microstructure_list[4: irregular, nucleated at prior γ grain boundaries and annealing twins
truth.structures.mod_A5.overall_structure: Modified processing: refined B2 stringer bands, reduced fraction (~11%), softer γ prominent; IG-B2 and GB-B2 similar precipitation.
truth.structures.mod_A5.microstructure_counts: B2 stringer bands ~11%; γ phase fraction higher
truth.structures.mod_A15.overall_structure: Similar to mod_A5 with coarsening due to longer annealing.
truth.structures.mod_A30.overall_structure: Further coarsening, best ductility due to softer γ dominance.

prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_cast.overall_st: Dendritic austenite matrix with interdendritic δ-ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_hr.overall_: Deformed austenite matrix containing lamellar B2 stringer bands and intragranular B2 at shear bands
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_hr.microstr: lamellar stringer bands spanning multiple austenite grains
elongated austenite grains with strain localization
nucleated at shear bands in austenite
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_cr.overall_: Pancake austenite with thinned B2 stringer bands and deformed IG-B2
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_cr.microstr: thinned lamellar stringer bands and deformed intragranular precipitates
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_a5.overall_: Partially recrystallized microstructure with URE-γ, RE-γ, GB-B2, fine IG-B2, and retained B2 stringer bands
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_a5.microstr: mixture of recrystallized equiaxed and unrecrystallized pancake grains
coarse GB-B2, fine IG-B2, and lamellar stringer bands
bimodal: fine RE-γ and coarse elongated URE-γ along rolling direction
heterogeneous nucleation at shear bands within URE-γ
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_a30.overall: Partially recrystallized microstructure with coarsened IG-B2 needles, larger RE-γ grains, and retained B2 stringer bands
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_std_a30.microst: needle-shaped IG-B2 by coalescence, coarse GB-B2, and lamellar stringer bands
RE-γ grain size increased with annealing time; URE-γ remains coarse and pancake-shaped
grown by mutual coalescence in shear bands during 30 min anneal
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_mod_a15.overall: Refined B2 stringer bands in austenite matrix with reduced crack susceptibility after modified rolling and annealing
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_mod_a15.microst: dominant matrix between thinner stringer bands
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_mod_a30.overall: Austenite-dominant microstructure with refined B2 stringer bands (~11%), reduced macrovoid nucleation sites
prediction.structures.sample_fe10al_mod_a30.microst: prominent softer matrix absorbing plastic strain

Dislocations released from softer γ localize at γ/B2 interfaces near grain boundaries, depleting GND in γ bulk, leading to YPE and reduced strain hardening exponent.
truth.interfaces.reg_A5.interface_notes[0].str: Strain concentrates in softer γ; B2 harder phase deforms less. KAM maps show higher misorientation in γ near interfaces.
truth.interfaces.reg_A5.interface_notes[1].sli: B2 non-shearable, dislocations loop around B2 particles (Orowan mechanism). Decohesion of B2 stringer band grain boundaries observed during tensile loading, leading to microcracks.
truth.interfaces.reg_A5.phase_evolution: During annealing, δ-ferrite → B2 stringer; new IG-B2 and GB-B2 precipitation. B2 stringer undergoes static recrystallization into bamboo-like grains. Strain localization at γ/B2 interfaces reduces n value.
strain partitioning between softer γ and harder B2
Strain concentration higher in γ; strain incompatibility leads to YPE; still dislocation localization at GB-B2 interfaces reduces GND in bulk γ.
truth.interfaces.mod_A5.interface_notes[0].str: With thinner B2 stringer bands and higher γ fraction, γ absorbs plastic strain preferentially, delaying crack propagation and decohesion.

prediction.interfaces.sample_fe10al_mod_a15.defect_: Dislocations released from softer γ localize around GB-B2 at γ/B2 interfaces near grain boundaries, depleting geometrically necessary dislocation density in bulk γ
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe10al_mod_a15.interfa: Strain partitioning between softer γ and harder B2 results in higher strain concentration in γ; stress concentrations within B2 stringer bands trigger decohesion at grain boundaries
Slip transfer impeded at nonshearable B2 particles causing Orowan looping; static recrystallization subdivides B2 stringer bands into lamellar segments with orientation mismatch
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe10al_mod_a15.phase_e: δ-ferrite undergoes order-disorder transition to B2 at 1125 °C during homogenization; B2 stringer bands form via hot and cold rolling; annealing causes static recrystallization of B2 stringers into bamboo-like grains and precipitation of IG-B2 and GB-B2

truth.properties.HR.mechanical.tensile_propert: Figure 5a shows engineering stress-strain curve for HR condition.
truth.properties.reg_A5.mechanical.tensile_pro: Stress-strain curve in Figure 5a; exact YS/UTS not reported in text, only toughness provided.
truth.properties.mod_A5.mechanical.tensile_pro: Engineering stress-strain curve in Figure 6a; shows higher strength for A5.
truth.properties.mod_A30.mechanical.tensile_pr: Figure 6a shows best ductility for A30 among modified samples.

truth.performance.mod_A30.comparative_performa: Toughness 1.72 kJ/mol vs 0.5-1.5 kJ/mol for TRIP steel, comparable or better.

truth.characterization_methods.HR.purpose: show B2 stringer bands, IG-B2 at shear bands
truth.characterization_methods.HR.key_findings: δ-ferrite transformed into lamellar B2 stringer bands; IG-B2 nucleates at shear bands in γ.
truth.characterization_methods.reg_A5.key_find: IG-B2 finer in A5; GB-B2 coarse; B2 stringer bands present.
truth.characterization_methods.reg_A30.purpose: identify crack initiation at grain boundaries of B2 stringer bands
truth.characterization_methods.reg_A30.key_fin: B2 stringer band recrystallized into bamboo-like grains; grain boundaries decohesion leads to microvoids and cracks.
truth.characterization_methods.mod_A15.key_fin: Both γ and B2 undergo plastic deformation; strain concentration higher in γ.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_8.key_findi: δ-ferrite fraction reduced from 27% to 19% after HR; B2 stringer band fraction 16% in regular, 11% in modified.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe10al_s: quantitative microstructure and phase fraction measurement
δ-ferrite fraction reduced from approximately 27% in as-cast to approximately 19% after hot rolling
microstructural imaging of precipitates and stringer bands
Intra-(IG-B2) and intergranular (GB-B2) precipitation; unrecrystallized pancake austenite; B2 stringer band morphology
solute distribution in B2 and austenite matrix
Fe, Mn, Al, Ni enrichment in B2; C depletion in B2 confirming intermetallic nature
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe10al_m: phase mapping, orientation mapping, and strain distribution
Co-deformation of γ and B2 stringer bands; KAM map shows higher strain localization in γ than B2; grain boundaries inside B2 stringer bands revealed
uniaxial tensile testing (Instron 5982, 100 kN, ASTM E8, RD, 1 mm min^-1)
Modified process achieves nearly 1 GPa yield strength with high elongation and toughness up to 1.72 kJ mol^-1 (238 J cm^-3)

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated phase diagram, phase fractions, and solute partitioning consistent with experimental observations and Ref.[15].
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: predict phase stability (liquid, δ, γ, κ-carbide)
Matches as-cast phases and order-disorder transition at 1125°C.
show δ-ferrite reduction and B2 formation
Indicates δ-ferrite transforms to B2 near eutectoid temperature 851°C.
equilibrium distribution of Al, Ni, Mn in γ and B2
Shows enrichment of Al, Ni, Mn in B2, depletion in γ.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Thermodynamic equilibrium calculations (pseudo-binary phase diagram, phase fraction versus temperature, solute partitioning)
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated liquid-to-δ-ferrite, peritectic, and eutectoid temperatures and solute partitioning trends consistent with experimental microstructure and prior literature
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Estimate yield strength contribution from intragranular B2 particles
Shear modulus G = 70 GPa; Burgers vector b = 0.26 nm; D_IG-B2 = 0.29 μm and f = 1.1% in RE-γ; D_IG-B2 = 0.2 μm and f = 2.6% in URE-γ
Total IG-B2 strengthening calculated as 66 MPa (23 MPa from RE-γ + 43 MPa from URE-γ)

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The precipitation strengthening by IG-B2 was calculated using Ashby–Orowan equation: σ_IG-B2 = (0.538 G b √f / D) ln(D/2b), with G=70 GPa, b=0.26 nm. For URE-γ with D=0.2 μm and f=2.6%, σ=43 MPa; for RE-γ with D=0.29 μm and f=1.1%, σ=23 MPa, total 66 MPa.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Strain hardening exponent n (Hollomon) for modified samples ranged 0.19–0.27, attributed to strain localization at γ/B2 interfaces depleting GND in γ bulk (64%).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Conventional processing gave unsatisfactory toughness (0.48–0.89 kJ/mol) and inconsistent strength-ductility; fracture initiated by macrovoids in thick B2 stringer bands and their decohesion/bamboo-like grain boundary cracking.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: High-speed HR (10 rpm) refined B2 stringer bands (from 16% to 11% fraction) and increased γ fraction, enabling strain absorption by softer γ and achieving delayed fracture, YS 1 GPa, elongation 30%, toughness up to 1.72 kJ/mol (238 J/cm³) comparable to TRIP steel.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: B2 phase composition determined as Fe(Ni,Mn)Al; Ni partitions into B2 substituting Fe-Al bonds with stronger Ni-Al bonds, stabilizing B2 and making it non-shearable.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The alloy design follows rule that 1 wt% Al addition in steel reduces density by ~1.2%; target density reduction of 10% could reduce approximately 6% vehicle mass.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Figure 1a shows liquid, δ, γ, and κ-carbide formations in pseudo-binary phase diagram; Figure 1b phase fraction variation with temperature; Figure 1c,d solute partitioning.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Figure 5a shows engineering stress–strain curves for HR, A10, A15, A30 (regular), with toughness inset table (values in kJ/mol or J/cm³).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Figure 6a shows modified samples stress–strain curves with improved consistency, toughness inset 1.66, 1.56, 1.72 kJ/mol for A5, A15, A30 respectively.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Figure 7c TEM after interrupted tensile at ε=0.05 shows deformed γ and B2; Figure 7d,e EBSD phase and KAM maps show strain localization at γ/B2 interface.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The as-cast microstructure contains approximately 27% δ-ferrite dendrites in an austenite matrix due to high Al content.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: After standard hot rolling, δ-ferrite fraction reduced from 27% to roughly 19%, forming lamellar B2 stringer bands.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Under modified high-speed hot rolling, B2 stringer band volume fraction reduced from approximately 16% to 11%, bringing softer austenite to prominence and improving crack resistance.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Toughness values derived from integrals under stress–strain curves: standard HR 0.48, A10 0.69, A15 0.85, A30 0.89 kJ mol^-1 (or J cm^-3); modified A5 1.66, A15 1.56, A30 1.72 kJ mol^-1.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The modified processed steel achieves nearly 1 GPa yield strength and total elongation reported as 28% in the abstract and 30% in the conclusions, with tensile toughness reaching 1.72 kJ mol^-1 (238 J cm^-3) comparable to or exceeding TRIP steel (0.5–1.5 kJ mol^-1 or 69–207.8 J cm^-3).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Intragranular B2 precipitates in unrecrystallized γ (0.2 μm, 2.6%) and recrystallized γ (0.29 μm, 1.1%) provide a total precipitation strengthening of 66 MPa via the Ashby–Orowan mechanism.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Figure 5a and Figure 6a present engineering stress–strain curves, but quantitative yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, and elongation values for individual samples are not explicitly tabulated in the provided text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: B2 stringer bands undergo static recrystallization during annealing into a bamboo-like grain structure, leading to grain boundary decohesion and premature failure when bands are thick.
